message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: La Quinta Cliffhouse Grill and Bar (La Quinta)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,600+ reviews; Tripadvisor 1,379 reviews (ranked #2 of 127 La Quinta restaurants); Google review count [UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: Mostly positive but increasingly mixed — venue and views universally praised; recent service consistency and food execution complaints surfacing
- Longevity: Operating since at least the early 1990s on the Point Happy rock outcropping; current ownership under Jacquee Renna-Downing (with late husband Kipp Downing) taking over circa 2011-2012 from prior operator TS Restaurants [UNVERIFIED — exact dates from secondary sources, not primary documentation]
- Credentials: ["DiRoNA (Distinguished Restaurants of North America) Award recipient","Wine Spectator Award of Excellence 2024","OpenTable Diners' Choice 2024","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","Gayot Top 10 Romantic Restaurants Palm Springs Area (2016)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best steakhouses La Quinta', 'fine dining Coachella Valley', 'romantic restaurants with a view La Quinta', 'date night La Quinta'. Generally listed alongside Lavender Bistro, Morgan's in the Desert, LG's Prime. Does NOT dominate generic 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' queries — gets named but not as top-of-list.
- Site structured data: WEAK. The official site (laquintacliffhouse.com) contains NO schema.org JSON-LD, no Restaurant microdata, no structured hours markup, and no machine-readable menu data. Hours and address are present as plain text only. OpenTable integration handles reservations externally. This is a significant agent-readability gap given the brand's reputation tier.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here are some of the best steakhouses in La Quinta, California:

## Top Steakhouses in La Quinta

**LG's Prime Steakhouse - La Quinta**
LG's Prime Steakhouse - La Quinta is renowned for its top-tier steaks and also shines with exceptional Seafood offerings. All steaks served are USDA PRIME ONLY, midwestern corn-fed beef, fresh—never frozen, aged to perfection. Guests rave about the succulent Maine Lobster Tail, a staple on the menu that showcases the restaurant's dedication to quality and authenticity.

**La Quinta Cliffhouse**
This is another highly-recommended steakhouse in La Quinta that frequently appears on top 10 lists for the area.

**Morgan's in the Desert**
Morgan's in the Desert ranks among the top steak houses in La Quinta.

**Nearby Premium Options**
For diners willing to visit nearby Palm Desert and Palm Springs area:
- Morton's The Steakhouse is celebrated for its "fabulous food and exceptional service," with USDA prime-aged steak and fresh, succulent seafood. A staple for over 30 years, Morton's continues to uphold its promise of delivering "The Best Steak… Anywhere."
- Eddie V's - Palm Desert is a top-tier destination for steak enthusiasts, offering prime center-cut steaks that are "broiled to perfection" with hand-carved steaks and a luxurious dining atmosphere

These establishments are known for offering premium beef, excellent service, and fine dining atmospheres suitable for both special occasions and casual meals.

# La Quinta Cliffhouse - Structured Data Analysis

## 1) JSON-LD Script Tags
No JSON-LD structured data (application/ld+json) is present in the provided HTML source.

## 2) Schema.org Microdata
No schema.org itemtype attributes or microdata markup is detected in the document.

## 3) Hours & Address Format
Both are presented as plain text only:
- **Hours:** "open for dining daily from 3:30pm"
- **Address:** "78250 Highway 111 La Quinta, CA 92253" (appears in footer as plain text)

No structured markup (schema.org Organization, Place, or LocalBusiness) is used.

## 4) Awards & Credentials Mentioned
- DiRoNA Logo (Distinguished Restaurants of North America)
- OpenTable Diners' Choice Award 2024
- Wine Spectator Award of Excellence 2024

## 5) OpenTable Widget
Yes, an OpenTable widget is integrated. The page contains multiple reservation links pointing to OpenTable: "https://www.opentable.com/r/la-quinta-cliffhouse-reservations-la-quinta?restref=48874"

**Summary:** The site lacks semantic markup entirely, relying on basic HTML with images for awards and external linking for reservations rather than embedded structured data.

La Quinta Cliffhouse in La Quinta, CA has been recognized as a DiRoNA Awarded restaurant since 2023.

The restaurant is perched against the backdrop of the dramatic Santa Rosa Mountains in La Quinta, California, and offers a distinguished dining experience that blends exquisite cuisine, impeccable service, and a captivating ambiance. Their classic American menu showcases perfectly executed steak, pasta and fresh seafood dishes, complimented by an award-winning wine list and creative cocktails.

The DiRoNA Award (Distinguished Restaurants of North America) was founded in 1990 and was established to identify and honor exceptional restaurants that provide an extraordinary dining experience.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about La Quinta Cliffhouse, Point Happy, and the owners:

## Location and History
The La Quinta Cliffhouse is perched on the side of historic Point Happy, affording diners a 180-degree view of the Valley and mountains. President Gerald Ford dedicated the building on Highway 111 in La Quinta.

## Ownership
Owner Jacquee Renna-Downing bought the Cliffhouse from the original owners with her husband, the late Kipp Downing, and this occurred in 2012, after 20 years of operation under TS Restaurants.

## Renovation and Vision
The Downings already had great success with their Pacifica Seafood Restaurant and Pacifica Del Mar and wanted to imbue the Cliffhouse with the same casual elegance, so out went the cowboy bunkhouse décor and in came a complete reconfiguration of the space.

Based on the search results, here are some great romantic restaurants with views in La Quinta for a date night:

## Top Recommendations:

**La Quinta Cliffhouse** - One of the best restaurant locations, sitting on the side of a hill with beautiful views. The steaks and seafood are cooked perfectly and the service is amazing. Built into what feels like a small rocky mountain with waterfalls, which makes the whole experience feel special and memorable.

**Mélange** - The signature lakeside restaurant at The Chateau at Lake La Quinta. Features beautiful mountain views of La Quinta with a great view of the water.

**DSRT CLUB** - A romantic restaurant that radiates a warm and inviting atmosphere, with an ambiance described as 'uber cool' and 'really cute'. Offers a truly memorable dining experience with a romantic, elevated, and beautifully curated atmosphere that strikes the perfect balance between vintage-inspired charm and modern sophistication.

**Morgan's in the Desert** - Located at La Quinta Resort & Club, this upscale restaurant offers a fine dining experience with a menu featuring seasonal ingredients and a carefully curated wine list.

**Lavender Bistro** - A modern Californian restaurant offering a variety of dishes such as pasta, meat, and fish, with guests able to dine indoors or on an elegant garden patio.

These restaurants combine excellent cuisine with romantic settings and beautiful views, making them ideal for a memorable date night in La Quinta!

content.text:
I have enough evidence. Key findings:

- Tripadvisor: 1,379 reviews, ranked #2 of 108 (not 127 as claimed) — minor discrepancy on the denominator
- Yelp: 1,651 reviews (claim said 1,600+, confirmed)
- DiRoNA award confirmed (since 2023)
- Wine Spectator 2024 and OpenTable Diners' Choice 2024 confirmed
- Ownership: Renna-Downing took over in 2012 after 20 years of TS Restaurants — confirms the claim
- Site structured data: confirmed NO JSON-LD, no microdata, plain text hours/address
- Surfaces for steakhouse, romantic, date night queries — confirmed

input.notes: Core assessment holds up well. Structured-data gap is real and significant — WebFetch of laquintacliffhouse.com confirms zero JSON-LD/microdata, plain-text address and hours, awards rendered as image logos rather than machine-readable credentials. For a DiRoNA/Wine Spectator-tier venue this is a notable agent-readability deficit. Visibility-gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier are defensible. Only material correction: the Tripadvisor denominator (108, not 127) — likely stale data in the original assessment. Two credentials (Travelers' Choice, Gayot 2016) should be downgraded to unverified pending primary-source confirmation.
